headline: A detached-backed BufferSource now fails closed with a typed error across the CBOR and WebCrypto input paths.
summary: A TypedArray whose backing ArrayBuffer has been transferred or structuredClone'd away is detached: reading its bytes throws. The toolkit's input paths coerce such a view with Buffer.from(view.buffer, view.byteOffset, view.byteLength), which on a detached buffer threw a raw TypeError that escaped the typed-error fail-closed contract every other malformed input honours. pki.cbor.decode and the pki.webcrypto digest / sign / verify / getRandomValues input coercion now catch that failure and reject with a typed error (cbor/not-buffer or webcrypto/data), threading the underlying view failure as the error cause, so a caller catches it with a single instanceof pki.errors.PkiError check like any other bad input. No API surface changes; hashing stays synchronous and no path is newly network-touching.

Fixed: pki.cbor.decode now rejects a detached-backed Uint8Array (a transferred / structuredClone'd view) with a typed cbor/not-buffer error instead of a raw TypeError from the byte walk.

The pki.webcrypto digest / sign / verify input coercion and getRandomValues now reject a detached-backed BufferSource with a typed webcrypto/data error instead of a raw TypeError. The underlying byte-view failure is threaded as the error cause on both paths.
